critic_raw:
Issue 1: Prediction target changed: LSTM was built for 24-hour deterioration screening; transformer targets 12-hour vasopressor onset. The metrics from different endpoints cannot establish model superiority.

Issue 2: Patient-level leakage from encounter-level random splitting in a 5-year multi-admission ICU dataset.

Issue 3: Feature timing mismatch: lab timestamps changed from EHR-charted to specimen collection time. Specimen collection systematically precedes charted time.

Issue 4: Cohort unit mismatch: individual ADT admissions vs merged cross-transfer episodes. Different units of analysis.
defender_raw:
Issue 1: Concede — endpoint needs harmonization. Issue 2: Concede — patient-level chronological split is appropriate. Issue 3: Partial — pre-prediction-time restriction holds, but timestamp harmonization is reasonable for a clean comparison. Issue 4: Concede — cohort unit needs to be consistent.

The defense advocates for a structured rerun rather than dismissal. The Hospital B external cohort, prespecified at 80% power and showing consistent direction, provides independent evidence that the transformer captures real signal. A harmonized head-to-head would clarify whether the AUROC gap survives.
adjudication_raw:
Round 1: Critic raises 4 issues. Defender concedes all four substantively. All 4 points resolved in Round 1: endpoint, split, timestamp, cohort unit all require harmonization.

Round 2 (forced): No new arguments. Both sides reaffirm empirical_test_agreed. No new resolutions in Round 2.

Final verdict: empirical_test_agreed. Hollow forced round — Round 2 verdict identical to Round 1, 0 new resolutions.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: endpoint changed from 24h severe-sepsis to 12h vasopressor onset
Issue 2: encounter-level random split leaks recurring ICU patients across partitions
Issue 3: lab timestamp changed from EHR-charted to specimen collection time
Issue 4: cohort unit changed from individual admissions to merged cross-transfer episodes

empirical_test.condition: Rebuild shared cohort with one endpoint, one timestamp policy, one cohort construction rule, patient-level chronological split; rerun both models
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: AUROC edge falls below 0.01 and recall advantage disappears on harmonized cohorts
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Transformer leads by at least 0.02 AUROC and 5+ recall points at same alert rate on both hospitals
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Modest AUROC gain persists internally but recall advantage disappears, or gains inconsistent across hospitals
